wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Hubora project overview section. Explain its purpose as a comprehensive media management and discovery platform, architecture overview, and relationship to other components. Document the key features including multi-format media cataloging, AI-powered discovery, cross-platform provider integration, and privacy-focused vault system. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like content discovery, library management, and social collaboration. Document the technology stack including React, TypeScript, Supabase, Netlify Functions, and Tailwind CSS. Include system requirements and installation prerequisites.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Hubora. Include step-by-step installation instructions, development environment setup, project structure explanation, and first contribution guidelines. Document the complete development workflow from cloning the repository to running the local development server. Explain the build process using Vite, deployment configuration with Netlify, and environment variable setup. Include troubleshooting common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Provide guidance on accessing the application locally and understanding the basic navigation structure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Hubora's system design. Describe the high-level architecture including the React frontend, Netlify Functions backend, Supabase database, and real-time data synchronization. Document component interactions, data flows between services, and integration patterns. Explain technical decisions such as the choice of Zustand for state management, service layer pattern implementation, and provider protocol architecture. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams showing how frontend components communicate with backend services and external APIs. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, caching strategies, and error handling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Hubora's core features. Explain the main functionality including content discovery engine, media library management, progress tracking, and social collaboration features. Document the user workflows for discovering new content, managing personal collections, tracking reading/watching progress, and sharing content with others. Include implementation details of the recommendation algorithms, search functionality, and real-time synchronization. Provide examples of common user scenarios and their corresponding technical implementations. Address performance optimizations and caching strategies used throughout the core features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Media Domain Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Hubora's media domain-specific features. Thoroughly explain the implementation of movies and TV shows management, reading platform with e-book support, gaming integration with achievement tracking, and adult content vault with privacy controls. Document the specific data models, UI components, and business logic for each media type. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how different media formats are handled, progress tracking implemented, and specialized features like the epub reader or game management modal work. Address format-specific considerations, file handling, and domain-specific validation rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Hubora's REST endpoints and internal services. For Netlify Functions, document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication methods. For internal services, document the service layer interfaces, method signatures, parameters, and return values. Include protocol-specific examples, error handling strategies, rate limiting considerations, and versioning information. Document the provider protocol for third-party integrations, authentication flow with Supabase, and real-time data synchronization patterns. Address security considerations, input validation, and response formatting standards used across all API endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for Hubora's database schema. Detail entity relationships between users, media items, progress tracking, social interactions, and provider configurations. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, constraints, and data types used throughout the schema. Explain data validation rules, business constraints, and referential integrity policies. Include database schema diagrams showing table relationships and data flow patterns. Document data access patterns, caching strategies at the application level, and performance considerations for large datasets. Specify data lifecycle management, backup procedures, and migration strategies for schema evolution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Provider Integration System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Hubora's pluggable provider integration system. Thoroughly explain the provider protocol specification, plugin architecture implementation, and third-party API integration patterns. Document the provider interface contracts, adapter implementations, and catalog management system. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how to implement custom providers for different media sources. Document the provider registration process, health monitoring, error handling, and fallback mechanisms. Address testing strategies for provider implementations, debugging techniques, and performance optimization for external API calls.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components & Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Hubora's UI component library and design system. Describe the visual appearance, behavior, and user interaction patterns of reusable components. Document props/attributes, events, slots, and customization options for each component. Include usage examples with code snippets showing proper implementation patterns. Provide guidelines for responsive design using Tailwind CSS, accessibility compliance, and theme customization. Document component states, animations, transitions, and composition patterns. Address cross-browser compatibility considerations and performance optimization techniques for large component trees.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation for Hubora's multi-layered testing strategy. Document unit testing approaches using Vitest, integration testing patterns for services and APIs, and end-to-end testing with Playwright. Explain test organization, fixture management, and mocking strategies for external dependencies. Include examples of testing React components, service layer functions, and user workflows. Document test coverage requirements, continuous integration setup, and performance testing approaches. Address testing best practices, debugging failed tests, and maintaining test reliability across different environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for Hubora. Document the build process using Vite, Netlify deployment configuration, and CI/CD pipeline automation with GitHub Actions. Explain environment variable management, database migrations, and production deployment strategies. Include service worker configuration for offline capabilities, performance optimizations, and monitoring setup. Document release processes, package management, and deployment verification procedures. Address scaling considerations, backup strategies, and disaster recovery procedures for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for contributing to Hubora. Document code standards including TypeScript best practices, React component patterns, naming conventions, and style guide adherence. Explain the development workflow, Git branching strategy, pull request process, and code review procedures. Include security guidelines, performance optimization tips, and debugging techniques. Document project structure conventions, file organization patterns, and documentation standards. Address common development pitfalls, troubleshooting approaches, and community contribution guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation for Hubora. Document common issues encountered during development, deployment, and usage with step-by-step solutions. Include debugging guides for frontend components, service layer errors, database connectivity issues, and API integration problems. Address performance troubleshooting, memory leaks, and browser compatibility issues. Document security considerations, vulnerability mitigation, and access control troubleshooting. Include frequently asked questions about feature usage, configuration options, and extension development. Provide diagnostic tools, log analysis techniques, and escalation procedures for complex issues.
